meta.title: Performance Optimization Case Study — News & Video PWA
meta.description: 10-day LinkedIn content plan with before/after Core Web Vitals metrics from a production content PWA.
postingTips: Post once daily at the same time (e.g. 9–10 AM) for best reach.
Attach a screenshot for every post — Lighthouse panels, DevTools LCP breakdown, or a before/after graphic.
Reply to comments within the first hour to boost visibility.
Day 1 is the hook; Days 2–8 are deep dives; Days 9–10 are lessons and wrap-up.

pagesTested.description: Home / news list — hero card image is LCP element
Long-form article with publisher metadata & ads
Short-form video page with YouTube embed & video ads

clsVideoDetail.notes: Already good; CLS was the blocker

lcp.title: Hero image preload in <head>
lcp.description: Injected a high-priority preload link for the first card image during server-side rendering.

lcp.title: HTTP Link preload header
lcp.description: Browser starts fetching the LCP image before the HTML body is parsed — earliest possible discovery.

lcp.title: CDN preconnect + dns-prefetch
lcp.description: Early TCP/TLS handshake to the image CDN saves another 200–300 ms on first paint.

lcp.title: Stable SSR image URL
lcp.description: Fixed deterministic dimensions so the preload URL matches the rendered after hydration — the preloaded asset is never wasted.
lcp.snippet: SSR image URL = client image URL (stable dimensions)

cls.title: font-display: optional + metric fallback
cls.description: Stopped post-paint custom font swaps on multi-language pages. Added a metric-adjusted system fallback with ascent/descent overrides.

cls.title: Hero image reserved space
cls.description: Pre-calculated minimum height from thumbnail aspect ratio prevents image-load layout jumps on article pages.

cls.title: YouTube click-to-play
cls.description: Poster thumbnail with 16:9 aspect-ratio box. YouTube iframe loads only on user tap — no late unsized inject.
cls.snippet:
Click-to-play poster → iframe loads on tap
aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; min-height reserved

cls.title: Floating ad slot reservation
cls.description: Fixed 320×100 dimensions on the floating ad wrapper before the creative renders.

timeline.title: LCP Preload + CDN Preconnect
timeline.description: Hero image preload in head, HTTP Link header, stable SSR URL — eliminated ~1400 ms resource load delay.

timeline.title: Global Image Lazy Loading
timeline.description: Below-fold images lazy-loaded; above-fold card keeps eager + fetchPriority high.

timeline.title: Detail Hero CLS + Ad Lazy Loading
timeline.description: Pre-calculated hero container, viewport-based ad lazy load, deferred video ad SDK.

timeline.title: Font CLS Fix
timeline.description: font-display: optional for multi-language fonts; metric-adjusted fallback; floating ad slot reservation.

timeline.title: YouTube Video CLS
timeline.description: Click-to-play poster mode, 16:9 aspect-ratio container, reserved hero wrapper on video detail pages.

days.title: The Big Picture — Core Web Vitals Wins
days.topic: Series opener · Hook post with all headline numbers
days.imageSuggestion: Single stats card or carousel slide showing all three metrics — LCP -44%, Article CLS -98%, Video CLS -92%.
days.linkedinPost:
Starting a 10-day series on web performance optimization 🚀

We audited a production news & video PWA with Lighthouse and fixed the worst Core Web Vitals offenders.

Here's what changed on mobile (production, simulated 4G):

⚡ LCP Resource Load Delay: 3,200 ms → 1,800 ms (-44%)
📐 Article Detail CLS: 1.321 → 0.02 (-98%)
🎬 Video Page CLS: 1.435 → 0.12 (-92%)
✅ Main-frame UI CLS: 0.017 (stable throughout)

Over the next 9 days I'll break down exactly how we got there — one optimization at a time.

Follow along if you care about LCP, CLS, and making ad-heavy PWAs actually fast.

Day 2 tomorrow: why your LCP image loads 3 seconds late 👇

#Lighthouse #CoreWebVitals #WebPerformance #PWA #Frontend #LCP #CLS

days.title: Why LCP Has a 3-Second Delay
days.imageSuggestion: Chrome DevTools LCP breakdown screenshot — highlight the 3,200 ms Resource load delay bar.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 2/10 — Your LCP image isn't slow. It's discovered too late.

On our content feed, Lighthouse showed this LCP breakdown:

• Time to first byte: ~0 ms ✅
• Resource load delay: 3,200 ms ❌
• Resource load duration: 1,230 ms
• Element render delay: 10 ms ✅

The image itself downloads fine. The browser just doesn't know about it until JavaScript runs and React hydrates.

That 3.2 s delay is pure waste — and it's the #1 LCP killer on SSR apps.

Tomorrow: the 3-part fix that cut it to ~1.8 s.

What's your biggest LCP bottleneck — images, fonts, or third-party scripts?

#WebPerformance #LCP #CoreWebVitals #Frontend #PWA

days.title: Preload the LCP Image Early
days.imageSuggestion: Network waterfall before/after — show image request starting earlier. Or a code snippet of the preload tag.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 3/10 — Tell the browser about your LCP image before JS runs.

Fix #1 for our 3,200 ms LCP delay: preload the hero card image in two places.

1️⃣ In the HTML <head>:
<link rel="preload" as="image" fetchpriority="high" href="…" />

2️⃣ As an HTTP response header (even earlier):
Link: <image-url>; rel=preload; as=image; fetchpriority=high

The header wins — the browser starts downloading before parsing a single line of body HTML.

Result: resource load delay dropped from 3,200 ms → ~1,800 ms.

Simple rule: if you know the LCP element at build/SSR time, preload it. Don't wait for React.

#WebPerformance #LCP #CoreWebVitals #Frontend #SSR

days.title: Don't Waste Your Preload
days.topic: Fix #2 · CDN preconnect + stable SSR URL
days.imageSuggestion: Side-by-side: wasted preload (different URL after hydration) vs fixed (same URL). Or preconnect tags screenshot.
days.linkedinPost: Day 4/10 — Your preload is useless if the URL changes after hydration. We found two more LCP killers hiding behind the preload: 🔌 CDN cold start (~200–300 ms lost) Fix: + dns-prefetch to your image CDN in 🔄 SSR ↔ client URL mismatch Server rendered the image at 379×213. After React hydration, client recalculated to 546×307. The preloaded file was completely wasted — browser fetched a new URL from scratch. Fix: compute one stable image URL on the server. Use the exact same URL in preload, src, and after hydration. Preload + wrong URL = expensive no-op. Have you checked if your SSR and client image URLs actually match? #WebPerformance #LCP #CoreWebVitals #Hydration #Frontend

days.title: Fonts Are Quietly Destroying Your CLS
days.imageSuggestion: Lighthouse CLS breakdown showing text elements shifting (0.652 each). Before/after CLS score: 1.321 → 0.02.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 5/10 — font-display: swap is killing your CLS score.

Article detail pages scored CLS 1.321 — 13× worse than Google's "good" threshold.

Lighthouse pointed at two text lines (publisher metadata). Root cause: custom language fonts loading after paint.

The sequence:
1. Page renders with Arial (fallback)
2. Custom font loads and swaps in
3. Text reflows → layout shift → CLS 0.652 per element

The fix:
• font-display: swap → optional (no post-paint swap)
• Metric-adjusted fallback font (ascent/descent overrides so Arial matches custom font dimensions)

Result: CLS 1.321 → ~0.02 (-98%)

If you serve non-Latin scripts, check your @font-face strategy today.

#CLS #CoreWebVitals #WebPerformance #WebFonts #Frontend

days.title: Reserve Space Before Ads Load
days.topic: CLS fix · floating ad slot dimensions
days.imageSuggestion: Screenshot of fixed bottom ad bar — highlight the reserved 320×100 slot vs unsized creative loading in.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 6/10 — Unsized ad slots = guaranteed layout shift.

Our floating bottom ad contributed CLS 0.017 — small alone, but fixable.

The problem: the ad wrapper had no fixed height. When the creative image loaded, the bar grew and pushed content up.

The fix: reserve the slot size before the ad request fires.

position: fixed; bottom: 0;
min-height: 100px; width: 320px;

Set dimensions from your ad slot config — not from whatever the creative happens to be.

This won't fix VPAID iframe shifts (that's a different beast), but it eliminates first-party ad CLS entirely.

Rule: every ad container needs width + height before the ad SDK runs.

#CLS #WebPerformance #AdTech #CoreWebVitals #Frontend

days.title: YouTube Embeds and CLS
days.imageSuggestion: Video detail page — poster thumbnail with play button (before click) vs late-loading iframe shift (before fix).
days.linkedinPost:
Day 7/10 — YouTube iframes are CLS bombs on video pages.

Video detail pages had a unique problem: the hero wasn't a static image — it was a YouTube embed that injected a late, unsized iframe.

What we did:

1️⃣ Reserved a 16:9 container with min-height from day one
2️⃣ Show a poster thumbnail + play button instead of loading the iframe immediately
3️⃣ Load YouTube only on user click

Before: unsized iframe injects → massive layout shift
After: stable poster box → iframe loads inside fixed space

Main-frame UI CLS stayed at 0.017 throughout.

Click-to-play isn't just better UX on mobile — it's a CLS strategy.

#CLS #CoreWebVitals #WebPerformance #YouTube #Frontend

days.title: Lazy-Load Ads, Delay Heavy Scripts
days.topic: Ad & script optimization · viewport lazy load
days.imageSuggestion: Diagram or bullet graphic: Eager load vs Lazy load when 300px from viewport. Performance score 32 → 58.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 8/10 — Stop loading ads the user can't even see yet.

Beyond CLS, ads were blocking the main thread and hurting the Lighthouse performance score (32 → 58 after fixes).

Three changes:

📦 Display ads → lazy load when entering viewport (300px margin)
📦 Programmatic supply ads → intersection observer, load once visible
⏱️ Video ad SDK → delayed 5 seconds after page load

Also split image loading strategy:
• Above-fold / LCP images → loading="eager" + fetchPriority="high"
• Everything below → loading="lazy"

Ads pay the bills. But eager-loading every ad slot on page load is expensive.

Lazy ads = faster LCP + less main-thread blocking + happier users.

#WebPerformance #AdTech #CoreWebVitals #LazyLoading #PWA

days.title: Total CLS vs Main-Frame CLS
days.topic: Key lesson · measuring what you can actually fix
days.imageSuggestion: Lighthouse CLS panel — total CLS 1.435 vs main-frame CLS 0.017 highlighted side by side.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 9/10 — Your CLS score might not be your fault.

This was the most important debugging lesson from the whole sprint:

Video page total CLS: 1.435 ❌
Main-frame CLS (our UI): 0.017 ✅

Almost all of the 1.435 came from third-party VPAID video ad iframes — each contributing ~0.709 shifts. Lighthouse often misattributes these to a nearby <script> tag.

Our own UI was stable the entire time.

What this means for you:
• Don't panic at a high total CLS on ad-heavy pages
• Always check main-frame CLS separately
• Fix what you control: fonts, image sizing, ad slot reservation, embed strategy
• Push ad partners on iframe sizing — or delay their scripts

You can't fix VPAID, but you can prove your app isn't the problem.

#CLS #CoreWebVitals #WebPerformance #VPAID #Lighthouse

days.title: Full Recap — 10 Days of Performance Wins
days.imageSuggestion: Carousel recap — one slide per metric (LCP, Article CLS, Video CLS, Perf score) or a single summary infographic.
days.linkedinPost:
Day 10/10 — 10 days of web performance optimization. Full recap 👇

We audited a production news & video PWA and fixed the worst Core Web Vitals issues. Here's everything in one place:

⚡ LCP delay: 3,200 ms → 1,800 ms (-44%)
   → Preload in head + HTTP Link header + CDN preconnect + stable SSR URL

📐 Article CLS: 1.321 → 0.02 (-98%)
   → font-display: optional + metric-adjusted fallbacks

🎬 Video CLS: 1.435 → 0.12 (-92%)
   → Click-to-play embeds + reserved 16:9 hero + lazy ads + delayed video SDK

📈 Performance score: 32 → 58–62 on video pages

💡 #1 lesson: measure main-frame CLS separately from VPAID iframe noise.

If this series was useful, save this post and share it with your frontend team.

What should I deep-dive next — INP optimization, RUM setup, or image CDN strategy?

Thanks for following all 10 days 🙏

#Lighthouse #CoreWebVitals #WebPerformance #PWA #Frontend #LCP #CLS #WebDev #PerformanceOptimization
